# Data Model: Selective Import (003)

**Branch**: `003-selective-import` | **Phase**: 1 — Design  
**Generated by**: speckit.plan

---

## Overview

This feature adds two fields to the existing `Integration` model and introduces a new `SubSource` value type (never persisted — used in API responses only). No new tables are required.

---

## Modified Model: `Integration`

### New Fields

```prisma
model Integration {
  // ... existing fields unchanged ...

  // Selective import — 003
  importEverything     Boolean  @default(true)
  selectedSubSourceIds String[] @default([])   // PostgreSQL text array; stable external IDs only
}
```

### Field Definitions

| Field | Type | Default | Nullable | Description |
|-------|------|---------|----------|-------------|
| `importEverything` | `Boolean` | `true` | No | When `true`, all items from this integration are synced regardless of `selectedSubSourceIds`. When `false`, only items whose sub-source ID is in `selectedSubSourceIds` are synced. |
| `selectedSubSourceIds` | `String[]` | `[]` | No (empty array) | Ordered list of stable external sub-source IDs selected by the user. Ignored when `importEverything = true`. Must not be empty when `importEverything = false` (enforced at API layer). |

### Validation Rules

- **INV-001**: If `importEverything = false`, `selectedSubSourceIds` MUST contain at least one element. Saving `importEverything = false` with an empty array is rejected (HTTP 400).
- **INV-002**: Individual IDs in `selectedSubSourceIds` are opaque strings — the backend does not validate them against the live provider. Stale IDs (deleted sub-sources) are silently ignored during sync.
- **INV-003**: When `importEverything` is toggled back to `true`, `selectedSubSourceIds` retains its last value; it is not cleared. This allows the toggle to be reversed without losing the previous selection.

### Migration

```sql
-- Migration: add_selective_import_to_integration
ALTER TABLE "Integration" 
  ADD COLUMN "importEverything" BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  ADD COLUMN "selectedSubSourceIds" TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}';
```

No backfill needed: `importEverything = true` (default) preserves existing sync behaviour for all current integrations (SC-004).

---

## Value Type: `SubSource`

Defined in `backend/src/integrations/_adapter/types.ts`. Never stored; returned live by `listSubSources()`.

```typescript
export interface SubSource {
  /** Stable external identifier from the provider (used as the persisted key). */
  id: string;
  /** Human-readable display name shown in the UI. */
  label: string;
  /** The category of grouping this sub-source represents. */
  type: 'list' | 'calendar' | 'label' | 'folder';
}
```

### Type Mapping Per Integration

| Integration | `type` value | ID source | Label source |
|-------------|-------------|-----------|-------------|
| Gmail | `'label'` | Gmail label `id` (e.g., `Label_123`) | Gmail label `name` |
| Apple Reminders | `'list'` | CalDAV collection href (URL path) | CalDAV `displayname` property |
| Microsoft Tasks | `'list'` | Graph API todoTaskList `id` (GUID) | Graph API `displayName` |
| Apple Calendar | `'calendar'` | CalDAV collection href (URL path) | CalDAV `displayname` property |

---

## Updated `IntegrationAdapter` Interface

Add optional method to `backend/src/integrations/_adapter/types.ts`:

```typescript
export interface IntegrationAdapter {
  // ... existing methods unchanged ...

  /**
   * Returns the available sub-sources for this integration.
   * Called by the API when the user opens the import filter panel.
   * Returns an empty array if the integration does not support sub-sources
   * or if the provider returns none.
   *
   * Optional — adapters that do not implement this fall back to "Import everything" only.
   */
  listSubSources?(integrationId: string): Promise<SubSource[]>;
}
```

---

## Updated `IntegrationStatusItem` (Service Layer)

`backend/src/integrations/integration.service.ts` — extend the return type:

```typescript
export interface IntegrationStatusItem {
  serviceId: ServiceId;
  status: IntegrationStatus;
  lastSyncAt: string | null;
  lastSyncError: string | null;
  gmailSyncMode: 'all_unread' | 'starred_only' | null;
  // New fields — 003
  importEverything: boolean;
  selectedSubSourceIds: string[];
}
```

---

## State Transitions

```
New integration connected
  → importEverything = true, selectedSubSourceIds = []
  → User opens filter panel → listSubSources() called
  → User selects sub-sources + toggles importEverything = false → PUT /import-filter
  → importEverything = false, selectedSubSourceIds = ["id1", "id2"]

User re-enables "Import everything"
  → PUT /import-filter { importEverything: true, selectedSubSourceIds: ["id1", "id2"] }
  → importEverything = true (selectedSubSourceIds retained, ignored during sync)

Integration disconnected
  → Integration record deleted (CASCADE) → all filter settings deleted with it
```

---

## Sync Filter Logic (per adapter)

Each adapter's `sync()` applies the filter after fetching items from the provider:

```typescript
// Pseudocode — implemented inside each adapter's sync()
async sync(integrationId: string): Promise<NormalizedItem[]> {
  const integration = await prisma.integration.findUnique({ where: { id: integrationId } });
  // ... existing token refresh / API call logic ...

  const allItems: NormalizedItem[] = /* ... fetch from provider ... */;

  if (integration.importEverything) {
    return allItems;
  }

  const allowed = new Set(integration.selectedSubSourceIds);
  return allItems.filter(item => allowed.has(item.subSourceId ?? ''));
  // Note: NormalizedItem will gain an optional subSourceId field (see contracts/api.md)
}
```

### `NormalizedItem` extension (internal — not exposed in API)

```typescript
export interface NormalizedItem {
  // ... existing fields ...
  /** The sub-source ID this item belongs to (used for filter; not exposed externally). */
  subSourceId?: string;
}
```

This field is set by each adapter during item construction and used only within `sync()` for filtering before returning.
